summary: Clean 1969 audience tape that captures a solid late‑'69 Dead set with standout jams on The Other One, China Cat > Rider and Baby Blue.
review: The recording is an unusually clear audience (AUD) capture for 1969, praised as “ultra clean” and sounding better than most early ’70 tapes. Musically the show delivers classic late‑'69 material – a ripping The Other One sequence, a killer China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider jam, plus memorable moments from Uncle John’s Band, Baby Blue and Dancing in the Street. While a few of the short songs feel a bit sloppy, the overall performance is considered a fun and essential snapshot of the band just before Altamont.

ai_rating.rationale: Reviews consistently highlight strong performances and an unusually good audience recording; weighting favors the musical quality (70%) with solid technical merit (30%).

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as a decent/ultra‑clean AUD for 1969, clearer than most 1970 tapes; no major dropouts or noise complaints.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows through classic late‑'69 jamming, with a strong The Other One sequence and extended jams that keep the momentum; occasional sloppiness on short numbers is noted but does not detract from the overall vibe.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Quoted as saying "Not only that but it's something to do" before Altamont, showing his presence in the tape’s lore.
